page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m1.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.m1 server family. Built on the x86_64 CPU architecture and running on the KVM hypervisor, this instance features 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing a total of 8 vCPUs. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures that compute resources are not shared with other tenants, offering consistent processing capabilities.

The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per physical core. The instance does not include any bundled local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning storage must be attached externally, and it is not designed for hardware-accelerated graphics or parallel computing workloads. Additionally, it does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given its balanced 1:4 CPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated core allocation, the ecs.m1.xlarge is designed for general-purpose workloads. It is suitable for applications such as mid-tier web serving, application servers, containerized microservices, and development environments that require stable CPU performance without the need for local high-speed storage or specialized GPU acceleration.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m1.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance within the ecs.m1 family, utilizing the x86_64 architecture and the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with 1 thread per core, ensuring consistent, non-shared compute performance. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include bundled local storage or GPU hardware, requiring external storage attachments for data persistence. Lacking specialized hardware accelerators or integrated storage, this instance is qualitatively positioned as a cost-efficient option for standard workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, including medium-scale web serving, application hosting, containerized deployments, and software development environments that demand dedicated CPU resources without extreme memory or storage requirements.
